description: Clean baseline for the solver3 objective autoresearch lane after the x100 metric-scale reset; per-case score metrics are the main anti-gaming signal.

Adaptive temperature calibration: catastrophically bad. Calibrated T_initial too high (~55 for balance cases), making search random. Also surfaced deeper bug: cooling schedule uses max_iterations=3M but only ~188K iterations complete in 15s, so temperature barely changes.

asi.hypothesis: Calibrate T_initial from positive delta samples to give 40% uphill acceptance
asi.rollback_reason: Primary 186.62 vs baseline 107.85. T_initial calibrated to ~55, T_final=1.375 → search never converges.
asi.root_cause_discovered: Cooling schedule mismatch: max_iterations=3M but only ~188K iterations in 15s → temperature stays near 2.0 throughout. T_initial=2.0 too low for balance penalties but adaptive calibration overshoots.
asi.next_action_hint: Switch to TIME-BASED cooling (elapsed/time_limit as progress) when time_limit is set. Keep T_initial=2.0, T_final=0.05 but let cooling happen in wall-clock space. This alone won't help balance case but fixes the cooling mismatch first.

description: Time-based cooling: max(iter_fraction, time_fraction) as SA progress. Fixes the schedule mismatch where max_iterations=3M but only ~188K iters complete in 15s. attribute_balance 1.404→0.995 (beats reference), synthetic_partial below reference too. Also 3x raw iteration speedup from debouncing Instant::elapsed every 64 iters.

asi.hypothesis: max(iter_progress, time_progress) drives SA cooling so whichever stop-condition binds ensures T reaches T_final
asi.key_finding: Iteration rate ~188K/15s vs max_iterations=3M meant T barely moved (stayed at 1.64 throughout). With time-fraction dominating, T now properly cools 2.0→0.05 over the budget. attribute_balance 1.404→0.995, primary 107.85→100.11
asi.speedup: Debouncing Instant::elapsed every 64 iters cut solver3_raw_score_us from 79.6 to 24.7 (3x)
asi.remaining_gaps: large_gender 1.015x, sailing_trip 1.017x — both within 2% of reference

description: T_initial=1.0, T_final=0.02: cooler start hurt sailing_trip (1.017→1.034) and large_gender (1.015→1.025). Sailing fixed-iteration also regressed to 1.089, confirming T=1.0 is too cold for cases that need exploration. Previous T=2.0 was better.

asi.hypothesis: T_initial=1.0, T_final=0.02 reduces random hot-phase exploration
asi.rollback_reason: 100.17 vs prev 100.11. sailing_trip 1.017→1.034, large_gender 1.015→1.025. Fixed-iteration sailing 1.089 confirms too cold. Cases needing escape from local optima need T≥2.0.
asi.next_action_hint: T_initial=2.0, T_final=0.05 is the best known. Try population-based restart or reheating when no_improvement > threshold to improve sailing/gender gaps within the existing T schedule.

description: Two-cycle incumbent restart (midpoint warm restart from best-so-far) regressed overall. It helped sailing_trip (1.017→0.992) but hurt attribute_balance (0.995→1.045) and fixed-iteration quality. The restart family is still promising, but unconditional midpoint restart is too disruptive.

asi.hypothesis: A second full cooling cycle from the incumbent best state can escape late local minima better than one long SA pass
asi.rollback_reason: Primary regressed 100.11→100.95. Attribute-balance and fixed-iteration quality both worsened materially. Unconditional midpoint restart is too blunt.
asi.mechanism_family_signal: Still promising as a restart/perturbation family because sailing_trip improved below reference while other cases regressed. Needs selective triggering, not mandatory midpoint restart on every case.
asi.next_action_hint: Incubation follow-up: make restart conditional on stagnation/no-improvement and only when current state has drifted meaningfully from best. Avoid restarting cases already converging well.

description: Conditional stagnation restart is much better than unconditional midpoint restart: fixed-iteration recovered to 100.0 and sailing stayed below reference, but primary still regressed vs best (100.11→100.23) because attribute_balance slipped from 0.995 to 1.015. Restart family still plausible with stricter trigger.

asi.hypothesis: Restart from incumbent-best only under stagnation can help hard local-minimum cases without forcing all cases through a second cooling cycle
asi.rollback_reason: Still worse than best: 100.11→100.23, mainly due to slight attribute_balance regression. Restart trigger is still too permissive.
asi.mechanism_family_signal: Directionally better than unconditional restart (100.95→100.23), so the family is not dead yet. Sailing and synthetic remain good; stricter trigger may preserve attribute_balance.
asi.next_action_hint: Final incubation follow-up: require larger drift-from-best and later progress window before restart, so only truly stuck runs reheat. If that still misses, retire restart family for now and move to a new neighborhood/move type.

description: Final restart-family incubation attempt with much stricter trigger still regressed (100.60). It preserved attribute_balance/fixed-iteration but lost the sailing benefit and slowed hotpaths badly. Restart-based perturbation is retired for now; next experiments should pivot to a genuinely new neighborhood/move type.

asi.hypothesis: A very strict stagnation-triggered incumbent restart might preserve already-good cases while still helping rare local-minimum cases
asi.rollback_reason: Primary 100.60 vs best 100.11. Sailing benefit disappeared while hotpath and runtime diagnostics degraded materially. Restart-family ROI is poor.
asi.mechanism_family_signal: After three variants, restart/perturbation does not look like the next best path for this lane. It can be revisited later, but should be deprioritized now.
asi.next_action_hint: Pivot to a true search-innovation experiment: implement a new compound move / neighborhood rather than more restart tuning.

description: Tried a richer best-of-k sampled neighborhood inside existing move families (k=3 for swap/transfer, k=1 for clique). This enlarged the local neighborhood and helped sailing, but regressed the primary fixed-time aggregate versus the 100.105 best due to runtime inflation (~48us raw score vs ~24.7 baseline) and losses on large_gender/social_golfer/synthetic.

asi.hypothesis: A small best-of-k random neighborhood inside swap/transfer would convert recent speed headroom into better local move quality and improve the fixed-time solver3 objective suite.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric regressed from the best kept run and hotpath/runtime cost roughly doubled, so the richer neighborhood was too expensive at k=3.
asi.next_action_hint: Incubate the neighborhood family in a cheaper form: try asymmetric draws (e.g. swap=2, transfer=1), selective best-of-k only when temperature is low or for one family at a time, or targeted candidate generation rather than brute extra previews.
observations: Fixed-time objective suite score 100.2793 was worse than the best kept 100.1055.
Sailing improved to 1.062, but large_gender fell to 0.985 and social_golfer/synthetic also slipped.
solver3_raw_score_us increased to 48.04, suggesting preview multiplication dominated the benefit.

description: Incubated the richer-neighborhood family with a cheap asymmetric variant: best-of-2 on swap only, transfer/clique unchanged. This still regressed the primary metric badly, driven by a huge sailing collapse, so greedy local preview selection appears harmful for this family even when the runtime overhead is moderated.

asi.hypothesis: A cheaper swap-only best-of-k neighborhood might retain the upside of richer local choice without the runtime inflation of sampling multiple transfer previews.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric worsened sharply to 112.6265, with sailing exploding to 1.8003 fixed-time and 1.9072 fixed-iteration, so the neighborhood family appears directionally wrong rather than merely too expensive.
asi.next_action_hint: Retire naïve best-of-k preview selection for now. If revisiting neighborhood enrichment later, make it structurally different: target specific conflict patterns, add diversity/randomization guards, or score compound moves rather than greedily picking the locally best sampled swap.
observations: Swap-only best-of-2 still increased solver3_raw_score_us to 34.15, but the main failure was search quality, not just runtime.
The damage concentrated in stretch_sailing_trip_demo_real while most other cases stayed near baseline.
This suggests local delta-greediness can steer solver3 into a bad basin on sailing-like instances.

description: Tried a lightweight exact-undo tabu memory for swap and transfer moves, blocking recent reverse moves while leaving clique swaps unchanged. This was a genuine metaheuristic change, but it still regressed the primary fixed-time aggregate versus the 100.105 best and increased solver overhead materially.

asi.hypothesis: A tiny tabu memory that blocks immediate reverse swap/transfer moves would reduce cycling and let solver3 spend more time exploring new basins, improving fixed-time objective quality.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric worsened to 101.0031 and runtime overhead rose notably, so exact-undo tabu did not pay for itself.
asi.next_action_hint: Retire simple reverse-move tabu for now. If revisiting search memory later, make it more state-aware: target repeated group-pair churn or repeated person-pair contact regressions rather than blanket move reversal bans.
observations: Large_gender, sailing, and synthetic all improved over reference, but not enough to beat the best kept aggregate because social_golfer slipped and runtime cost increased.
solver3_raw_score_us rose to 39.82 and clique/transfer path timings also increased, so even lightweight policy checks had a noticeable search cost.
This suggests solver3's current bottleneck is not simple immediate move reversal cycling.

description: Replaced simulated annealing with Late Acceptance Hill Climbing using a rolling 256-score history as the acceptance reference. This was a true non-SA driver, but it badly destabilized transfer attribute balance and large_gender, so the pure LAHC policy is not suitable in this form for solver3's canonical fixed-time bundle.

asi.hypothesis: A late-acceptance driver would escape SA's temperature-shape biases and improve basin traversal under fixed-time runs with modest overhead.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric regressed sharply to 106.4029, with especially bad damage on transfer attribute balance and large_gender, indicating that pure late-acceptance acceptance is too permissive or misaligned for solver3's penalty landscape.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not pursue pure LAHC unchanged. If revisiting non-SA acceptance, try a guarded variant: threshold acceptance with a shrinking cap, record-to-record travel, or a hybrid gate that only allows uphill moves when they stay within bounded regression on constraint-heavy penalties.
observations: Attribute-balance case worsened to 1.2525 fixed-time and 1.2794 fixed-iteration.
Large_gender worsened materially while sailing slightly improved versus reference.
Runtime overhead also increased to solver3_raw_score_us 36.07, so the driver was neither better nor cheaper.
asi.mechanism_family: non-SA acceptance / late acceptance hill climbing

description: Incubated the adaptive move-family bandit with a cost-aware UCB score that penalized expensive families by average family time. This variant catastrophically regressed the primary metric, largely by blowing up sailing, so this cost-aware bandit formulation is decisively not a keep.

asi.hypothesis: A cost-aware UCB family selector would preserve the upside of adaptive move-family allocation while avoiding over-spending on expensive families such as clique previews.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric worsened dramatically to 115.6401 and sailing exploded to 1.9590, indicating that the cost-aware bandit pushed solver3 into a very bad search regime.
asi.next_action_hint: Retire this cost-aware UCB variant. If online family adaptation is revisited, use richer reward signals tied to best-score improvements or case-agnostic structural conflict reduction, not simple improving-accept counts with cost penalties.
observations: The previous plain UCB family selector was mildly bad; adding cost awareness made the family much worse rather than better.
solver3_raw_score_us ballooned to 68.34 despite the intent to control cost.
The failure concentrated in sailing, suggesting the selector starved or mis-sequenced the move families that keep that landscape stable.

name: Solver3 fixed-time objective-quality autoresearch (x100 scale) [new-machine baseline reset]

description: Baseline reset attempt failed immediately because ./autoresearch.sh was not executable on this machine (exit 126: Permission denied).

asi.hypothesis: Establish a fresh solver3 objective-quality baseline on the new machine using the current kept record-to-record code.
asi.rollback_reason: Baseline run could not start because the entrypoint lacked execute permission on this machine.
asi.next_action_hint: Mark autoresearch.sh executable or invoke it via bash, then rerun the baseline so the new-machine noise floor is recorded.

description: Baseline reset still failed: the root wrapper now ran, but its delegated tools/autoresearch/solver3-objective-quality/autoresearch.sh script is also non-executable on this machine.

asi.hypothesis: Rerun the new-machine baseline via bash wrapper after fixing the top-level entrypoint assumption.
asi.rollback_reason: The delegated solver3 autoresearch wrapper inside tools/autoresearch/solver3-objective-quality/ also lacked execute permission, so the baseline still could not start.
asi.next_action_hint: Repair execute permissions for the nested autoresearch wrapper (or invoke the nested script via bash as well), then rerun the baseline.

description: New-machine baseline run completed and produced valid metrics, but the run must be discarded because autoresearch.checks.sh delegated into a non-executable tools/autoresearch/solver3-objective-quality/autoresearch.checks.sh wrapper on this machine.

asi.hypothesis: Record a fresh current-code baseline on the new machine before continuing solver3 search experiments.
asi.rollback_reason: The benchmark itself passed, but the required post-run checks could not execute because the delegated checks wrapper lacked execute permission on this machine.
asi.next_action_hint: Mark both autoresearch checks wrappers executable, rerun the same baseline, then start new search experiments from that clean new-machine segment.
asi.machine_reset: User explicitly requested a new baseline because this machine differs from the previous setup.
observations: The current kept record-to-record solver landed at objective_suite_weighted_normalized_score=102.06579338872064 on this machine.
This is materially slower than the previous machine: solver3_raw_score_us=79.59, with path and representative diagnostics also higher.
Quality shape stayed plausible: sailing and social_golfer beat reference, while attribute_balance, large_gender, and synthetic regressed.

description: Fresh solver3 objective-quality baseline on the new machine after fixing missing execute bits on the autoresearch wrapper scripts. Current kept record-to-record baseline is 101.3513 here; slower machine profile shows solver3_raw_score_us 77.30 with strongest gains in sailing/social_golfer and biggest gaps in attribute_balance, large_gender, and synthetic.

asi.hypothesis: Reset the autoresearch lane onto a clean new-machine baseline before further solver3 search changes.
asi.machine_reset: User explicitly requested a new baseline because this machine differs from the previous setup.
asi.environment_fix: Restored executable permissions on autoresearch wrapper/check scripts so the benchmark/check pipeline can run normally on this machine.
asi.baseline_observation: New-machine baseline is 101.35127940997147, materially worse than prior-machine history, so old noise/confidence should not be reused.

description: Front-loaded elite-scout multi-start crashed during the canonical suite: benchmark aggregation reported `case stretch.sailing-trip-demo-real has no final_score`, so at least one solver3 run failed to emit a valid completed case result under the new scouting orchestration.

asi.hypothesis: Use a short elite-scout phase (current state + three diverse seeded starts) to pick a better basin, then spend the remaining fixed-time budget on the standard record-to-record search.
asi.rollback_reason: The suite aborted because sailing-trip-demo-real produced no final_score after the new scouting orchestration, indicating a hard integration bug rather than a fair quality regression.
asi.next_action_hint: Inspect the failing suite log/report for sailing-trip-demo-real; likely causes are zero/invalid remaining time budget handling, missing result emission after the scout phase, or a solver error triggered by seeded reinitialization.
asi.mechanism_family: smarter elite scouting / concentrated multi-start portfolio

description: Noise-floor rerun on the new-machine baseline: slightly worse than the fresh baseline (101.3513→101.6588), so discard. This confirms the lane now has visible machine variance, with especially noisy raw-runtime/path diagnostics (solver3_raw_score_us 77.30→99.86, path_allowed_sessions_iter_us 25.15→81.84) even while objective quality stayed within ~0.31 points.

asi.hypothesis: Re-running the unchanged post-reset baseline will establish a new-machine noise floor before more ambitious solver3 changes.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric did not improve; the rerun was slightly worse and is best treated as variance measurement rather than a keep.
asi.next_action_hint: Use ~0.3 objective-score movement as an initial rough noise band on this machine and prefer experiments that target larger shifts or show consistent per-case wins; next real code experiment should be a lower-risk structural change than the crashed elite-scout orchestration.
observations: Per-case shape was almost unchanged versus the kept baseline.
Raw runtime diagnostics were much noisier than objective quality on this machine.
This supports doing at least occasional confirmation reruns before keeping marginal improvements.

description: Front-loaded elite-scout constructor portfolio with honest time accounting regressed catastrophically on the canonical bundle. Even after fail-soft skipping invalid diversified seeds, sailing exploded to 1.837x reference and large_gender worsened to 1.101x, so this light scouting form is not a viable next step.

asi.hypothesis: Spend a small front-loaded portion of the fixed-time budget on several short seeded scout runs, keep the best scout incumbent, then spend the remaining honest budget on the standard record-to-record search.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric collapsed from the new-machine baseline 101.3513 to 116.5707, dominated by a severe sailing failure and worse large_gender.
asi.next_action_hint: Retire light constructor-portfolio scouting for now. Pivot to a different top-queue family that changes the search mechanics more fundamentally, likely targeted ALNS/LNS repair or a stronger constructor rather than more seeded scouting.
asi.sanity_bug_learned: Some diversified constructor seeds are invalid on sailing due clique-capacity normalization; fail-soft skipping avoids crashes but does not rescue the search family.

description: Targeted two-group exact repair kernel was benchmark-honest but essentially inert. The occasional stagnation-triggered restricted repair over a session/group-pair subproblem left the aggregate basically unchanged versus the new-machine baseline (101.3513→101.3785), with small case reshuffling only.

asi.hypothesis: A tiny targeted ALNS/LNS step—exact repartition over a stagnated two-group subproblem with immovables fixed and full oracle rescoring—would unlock local improvements the move-by-move search misses.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric did not improve and the delta (+0.0272) is well inside the new-machine noise band, so this exact-repair micro form is too weak to justify keeping.
asi.next_action_hint: Retire this tiny two-group restricted repair form, but keep the broader ALNS/LNS family alive. Next try should use a larger hotspot destroy set or pivot to the still-untried top-queue memetic/GRASP families rather than more micro repair tuning.
observations: Sailing stayed good (0.9687x), but large_gender and synthetic remained the same weak spots as baseline.
The mechanism appears to have had little or no practical activation/coverage under the current trigger and neighborhood size.
This is a family-level signal that the destroy/repair region must be larger or better targeted to matter.

description: Structural tabu keyed to session/group-pair corridors was catastrophically bad. The coarse memory blocked too much legitimate revisitation, blowing up sailing to 1.875x reference, regressing social_golfer, and making representative/path hotpaths unusably slow.

asi.hypothesis: A coarse structural tabu over session/group-pair churn would suppress local corridor cycling better than exact-undo tabu and help the record-to-record search escape repeated structural loops.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary collapsed from the new-machine baseline 101.3513 to 118.3136, with major quality damage and extreme raw-runtime/path regressions.
asi.next_action_hint: Retire coarse group-pair structural tabu. The remaining strongest untried paths are now the more architectural ones: elite-population memetic search, a genuinely stronger GRASP-style constructor, or macro-operator hyper-heuristics—not more blanket memory bans.

description: First memetic-style follow-up on the new machine: added a one-shot stagnation-triggered offspring burst that briefly polishes a donor from a diversified seed, then transplants the donor's best repeat-pressure session into the incumbent before resuming record-to-record search. Primary improved slightly (101.3513→101.2810), but the gain is small and should be confirmed with a rerun because it is still within the current machine noise band.

asi.hypothesis: A lightweight memetic offspring burst using an elite incumbent recipient and a briefly polished diversified donor can improve search basin quality without the catastrophic budget fragmentation of full front-loaded multi-start.
asi.machine_noise_context: Current rough noise band on this machine is ~0.3 objective-score points, so this first gain is promising but not yet conclusive.
asi.mechanism_family: memetic / elite polished donor session transplant
asi.structural_change: During prolonged stagnation, generate one donor via short inner search, pick the donor session with the best local repeat-pressure improvement, transplant it into the incumbent, then continue the main search honestly within the same wall-clock budget.
asi.next_action_hint: Immediately rerun the same committed memetic-burst code once or twice to test whether the small primary improvement survives noise before incubating further.

description: Confirmation rerun of the memetic donor-session burst did not reproduce the slight gain from the first run. Same committed code landed at 101.4529 vs the kept 101.2810 and baseline 101.3513, so the current local-repeat-pressure donor-session selector looks too noisy to trust.

asi.hypothesis: Re-running the committed memetic-burst code will show whether the initial 101.2810 improvement is repeatable on this machine.
asi.rollback_reason: The confirmation rerun regressed relative to the memetic keep and also landed above the baseline, so the current session-selection rule is not yet robust.
asi.next_action_hint: Stay within the memetic incubation window but strengthen compatibility selection: score all candidate donor-session transplants by actual offspring score (or a richer compatibility heuristic) instead of choosing by local repeat-pressure gain alone.
asi.mechanism_family: memetic / elite polished donor session transplant

description: Memetic follow-up inside the incubation window: replaced the donor-session selector's cheap local repeat-pressure proxy with exact offspring rescoring across all allowable donor-session transplants, then accepted the best offspring under the existing record-to-record gate. This improved the primary metric from 101.2810 to 101.0558, with better transfer-balance and slightly better sailing while holding social_golfer and large_gender flat versus the prior memetic keep.

asi.hypothesis: A memetic donor burst becomes more robust if donor-session compatibility is evaluated by actual offspring score rather than a noisy local repeat-pressure proxy.
asi.mechanism_family: memetic / elite polished donor session transplant
asi.structural_change: During stagnation, polish one diversified donor briefly, enumerate all allowed one-session donor transplants into the incumbent, fully rescore each offspring, and inject only the best-scoring offspring back into the main record-to-record search.
asi.next_action_hint: Confirm this stronger memetic selector with at least one rerun; if it holds, continue incubation by testing richer donor compatibility or two-session offspring only if they remain benchmark-honest within the same time budget.

description: Confirmation rerun of the exact-rescored memetic donor-session burst stayed directionally positive versus the old baselines but did not beat the current memetic keep. The rerun landed at 101.1196, which supports that the stronger selector is materially better than the earlier proxy-based variant, but the best observed result remains 101.0558.

asi.hypothesis: The exact-rescored donor-session memetic burst should reproduce as a real improvement family rather than a one-off noise artifact.
asi.rollback_reason: The rerun was worse than the current keep (101.0558), so there is no new primary improvement to keep from this cycle.
asi.next_action_hint: Retain the kept exact-rescored memetic selector and use the next incubation turn on a richer but still budget-honest compatibility follow-up, or switch to the next untried large family if memetic follow-ups start plateauing.

description: Memetic follow-up that greedily allowed up to two exact-rescored donor-session transplants in one burst regressed badly. The second transplanted session appears to overfit the donor structure and especially hurt transfer-balance, so the current exact-rescored one-session offspring remains the better kept shape.

asi.hypothesis: A budget-honest greedy second donor-session transplant could unlock a stronger memetic offspring than the kept one-session exact-rescored burst.
asi.rollback_reason: Allowing a second transplanted donor session materially worsened the primary metric, mainly via transfer-balance regression, so this richer offspring shape is not worth keeping in its current form.
asi.next_action_hint: Retire naive greedy two-session extension inside this memetic line; if memetic incubation continues, prefer better donor compatibility or multi-donor choice rather than stacking extra donor sessions onto one offspring.
